purpose: Decision-critical reconciliation of royalty inventory, water economics, and emerging options using current primary disclosures.

primary_sources.role: FY2025 acreage, segment, investment, and project disclosure
Q1 2026 balance sheet and operating update

resolved_facts: FY2025 Form 10-K discloses approximately 882,000 surface acres, 1/128 NPRI under approximately 85,000 acres, 1/16 NPRI under approximately 371,000 acres, and approximately 33,000 additional NRA normalized to 1/8, totaling approximately 224,000 NRA.
FY2025 Form 10-K identifies Land and Resource Management and Water Services and Operations as reportable segments and separately identifies produced-water royalties in the revenue disaggregation.

TPL invested $50 million in Bolt in December 2025 and received equity, warrants, a water-supply right of first refusal, and a possible land-for-equity mechanism subject to mutual agreement; Bolt was still pursuing commercial partnerships and anchor customers.

The Phase 2B desalination facility has initial capacity of 10,000 barrels per day; construction was paused during 2025 and was anticipated to finish by the end of first-half 2026. This is development-stage evidence, not contracted cash flow.

partially_answered.finding: Primary filings identify produced-water royalties separately from water sales and services, supporting separate valuation components. Public disclosure still does not provide the complete volume-price-margin-capital bridge and transfer-pricing reconciliation required by the acceptance test.

Bolt and Phase 2B have identifiable contractual or physical milestones, but neither disclosure supplies a complete enforceable customer, ownership, remaining-capital, probability, and time-to-cash tree.

unresolved_material_facts: No public tract- or county-level roll-forward maps the 224,000 NRA into producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant non-overlapping inventory with royalty interests and development cadence.

The filing does not reconcile produced-water royalty and operated-water economics to volume, realized price, direct cost, maintenance capital, growth capital, ownership, and transfer pricing in one reproducible bridge.
No executed anchor-customer agreement, committed load, land contribution, ownership waterfall, or complete remaining-capital schedule supports a positive data-center option value.
Phase 2B completion, demonstrated unit economics, permitting, commercial customer terms, and remaining owner capital remain unresolved.
valuation_controls: Treat the 224,000 NRA disclosure as total ownership inventory, not as incremental undeveloped acreage.
Do not add water infrastructure replacement cost to royalty and service DCF values; use it only as an embedded cross-check.
Keep Bolt and desalination values probability-weighted and milestone-gated; neither is recurring contracted cash flow as of the evidence date.
conclusion: The current filing evidence improves classification and overlap control but does not satisfy any of the three follow-up acceptance tests. All remain open and the IC should remain evidence-blocked.
